nodes.context: Transnational organized crime syndicates originating in Japan, known for strict codes and rituals.
One of the principal clans of the Yakuza, with bosses from Kyushu.

A designated yakuza group in Fukuoka Prefecture, especially dangerous.

Largest yakuza syndicate in Japan, based in Kobe.
Splinter group from Yamaguchi-gumi, formed in August 2015.
Splinter group from Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi, formed in April 2017.
Splinter group from Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi, formed in July 2020.
Major yakuza syndicate, one of the three major organizations.

Yakuza syndicate heavily involved in drug trafficking.

Yakuza group headed by Tadamasa Goto, involved in US operations.
A major yakuza syndicate in Japan, targeted by anti-yakuza laws.
A yakuza group; its chief Kiyoshi Takayama was arrested in 2010.
A yakuza syndicate; its head was held liable for fraud in 2021.

edges.evidence_quote: Fukuoka Prefecture has the largest number of designated yakuza groups among all of the prefectures, at 5; the Kudo-kai, the Taishu-kai, the Fukuhaku-kai, the Dojin-kai, and the Namikawa-kai.
edges.context: Kudo-kai is a designated yakuza group, a subset of Yakuza.

edges.evidence_quote: Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi split off from Yamaguchi-gumi in August 2015
edges.context: Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi is a splinter group that split from Yamaguchi-gumi.

edges.evidence_quote: Kizuna-kai split off from Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi in April 2017
edges.context: Kizuna-kai is a splinter group that split from Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi.

edges.evidence_quote: Ikeda-gumi split off from Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi in July 2020
edges.context: Ikeda-gumi is a splinter group that split from Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi.

edges.evidence_quote: These Yamaguchi-gumi and the three organizations that split from them are fighting each other.
edges.context: Yamaguchi-gumi and Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi are in conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: the three major yakuza syndicates have formed a loose alliance, and in April 2023, Kiyoshi Takayama ... Shuji Ogawa ... and Kazuya Uchibori ... held a social gathering.
edges.context: Yamaguchi-gumi and Sumiyoshi-kai are part of a loose alliance among major yakuza syndicates.

edges.evidence_quote: the three major yakuza syndicates have formed a loose alliance, and in April 2023, Kiyoshi Takayama ... Shuji Ogawa ... and Kazuya Uchibori ... held a social gathering.
edges.context: Yamaguchi-gumi and Inagawa-kai are part of a loose alliance among major yakuza syndicates.

edges.evidence_quote: the three major yakuza syndicates have formed a loose alliance, and in April 2023, Kiyoshi Takayama ... Shuji Ogawa ... and Kazuya Uchibori ... held a social gathering.
edges.context: Sumiyoshi-kai and Inagawa-kai are part of a loose alliance among major yakuza syndicates.

edges.evidence_quote: they also established an alliance with the Brazilian drug cartel Primeiro Comando da Capital (PCC).
edges.context: Yamaguchi-gumi faction leader was involved in an alliance with PCC in Brazil.

edges.evidence_quote: Yamaguchi-gumi's number two and Kodo-kai chief Kiyoshi Takayama was arrested in late 2010.
edges.context: Kodo-kai is a sub-group or faction within Yamaguchi-gumi, as its chief is the number two of Yamaguchi-gumi.
